author: codex-jellyfin

patch.value: Enforce docstring and static analysis quality gates

Require complete high-quality source API documentation and strict non-bypassable checks for TypeScript safety, docstrings, duplication, pm metadata, dependencies, containers/filesystems, shell scripts, PowerShell, CodeQL/Greptile, and coverage reporting.

Every exported and internal source declaration has accurate actionable API documentation and docstring coverage is 100 percent; No any, inline/dynamic imports, non-erasable TypeScript syntax, or untyped external APIs remain; ESLint, jscpd, pm validation/lint, Trivy, ShellCheck, PSScriptAnalyzer where applicable, CodeQL, Greptile, and Codecov are mandatory documented gates; All tools are pinned, free to run in GitHub, and cannot be silently skipped when applicable files exist
Turn project quality policy into executable and reviewable guarantees.
Prevents undocumented, duplicated, insecure, or weakly typed changes from entering main.
A strict local and CI quality pipeline with 100 percent documentation evidence.
The new mandatory policy expands the release contract beyond runtime correctness and coverage.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Applicability must be explicit: ShellCheck/PSScriptAnalyzer gates fail on violations when matching scripts exist and report a deterministic no-files result otherwise.

message: Track mandatory documentation and static quality gates

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Claim mandatory documentation and static analysis gates

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Begin repository-wide TypeScript policy and gate implementation

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Current policy scan found two inline type imports and one parameter-property class; no explicit any types or dynamic imports were found in src. Existing ESLint treats no-explicit-any and unused declarations as warnings and disables the any rule in tests, so it is not yet a mandatory gate.

author: codex-jellyfin
message: Connect strict quality gates to docs runtime fixes and prior CI lineage

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Current quality evidence: typecheck and strict ESLint pass; every source file is at or below 300 code lines excluding comments; tracked-tree and repository-history secret scans pass. jscpd reports a 5.78% duplicated-line baseline, so zero-duplication and the complete mandatory gate bundle remain open work.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Strict TypeScript and JSDoc lint, source LOC, and both secret scans pass. The zero-duplication command is now explicit as check:duplicates, but remains red at the measured 5.78% baseline and is intentionally not represented as a completed release gate.

value.author: codex-jellyfin
value.text: Dependency remediation completed for the current lane: compatible overrides pin vite 8.1.5, flatted 3.4.2, postcss 8.5.21, esbuild 0.28.1, and picomatch 4.0.5; both bun audit and npm audit now report zero vulnerabilities. Typecheck, lint, and all 903 non-live tests still pass.

value.note: Mandatory type-safety erasable-syntax and JSDoc rules
Strict lint plugins duplication command and audited transitive overrides

value.command: bun run typecheck && bun run lint

patch.value: Reproducible audited npm compatibility dependency graph

value.note: Strict lint plugins duplication command and audited transitive overrides
